title: Review Evaluation of Residuals of TBI - Research-Derived Non-Public DBQ Substitute

public_availability.status: Not available for public use according to VA public DBQ inventory
public_availability.caveat: This is not an official VA DBQ and should not be presented as one. It is a structured research aid built from public VA/eCFR criteria and adjacent public examination concepts.

source.agency: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs / eCFR
source.research_method: Derived from VA public non-available DBQ list, public DBQs with similar exam structure, and 38 CFR Parts 3 and 4 criteria.

purpose: Prepare for review/increase TBI residuals evaluation where VA restricts the official DBQ.

diagnosis_current_condition: What condition, diagnosis, residual, or claimed symptom pattern is currently present?
When did it begin, who diagnosed or treated it, and what medications, devices, procedures, therapy, testing, or hospitalizations are documented?
What current objective findings, records, labs, imaging, audiology, neuropsychological testing, or clinician observations support it?
nexus_service_connection: What in-service event, injury, illness, exposure, captivity, deployment, hazardous duty, blast, acoustic trauma, or aggravation is claimed?
What dates, locations, units, MOS/duties, incidents, STR entries, personnel records, buddy statements, or exposure records support the event?
If secondary or aggravated, what service-connected condition caused or worsened this condition, and what medical evidence supports that relationship?
functional_impact_severity: How does the condition affect work attendance, task performance, concentration, communication, mobility, self-care, sleep, relationships, and daily activities?
What are baseline limitations versus flare-up or episode limitations?
Use truthful examples with frequency, duration, severity, triggers, treatment response, and missed/modified work.

rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.frequency_duration: How often symptoms/episodes occur and how long they last.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.severity_scale: Best, typical, and worst severity; use 0-10 only when clinically sensible, especially pain.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.work_daily_life_impact: Concrete examples such as missed work, accommodations, difficulty hearing speech, memory problems, lifting/sitting/standing limits, ADLs, or relationship effects.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.flare_ups: Triggers, frequency, duration, what worsens, what functional loss occurs, and what helps.

diagnosis_current_condition: Identify already service-connected TBI and all current residuals since the last exam/rating.
Describe worsening, improvement, new residuals, treatment changes, medications, rehab, hospitalizations, and new testing.
Differentiate TBI residuals from unrelated conditions when possible, while noting overlap that requires examiner judgment.
medical_history_onset_course_treatment: Timeline since last VA exam/rating: symptoms, treatment, testing, work changes, accidents, hospitalizations, and new diagnoses.
nexus_service_connection_prompts: For increases/reviews, focus on current severity and progression; nexus may already be established unless a new residual or secondary issue is claimed.
symptoms_signs_findings: Change in memory, concentration, executive function, judgment, orientation, social interaction, visual-spatial skills, communication, or consciousness.
Headaches, dizziness, vertigo, balance problems, sleep disturbance, fatigue, tinnitus, light/noise sensitivity, vision symptoms.
Irritability, impulsivity, mood swings, anxiety/depression, aggression, apathy, social withdrawal, or inappropriate behavior.
Seizures, motor/sensory deficits, gait/coordination issues, speech/swallowing problems, autonomic/endocrine residuals.
Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and functional loss of residual episodes since last evaluation.
diagnostic_testing_objective_findings: Review prior TBI exam/rating, current treatment records, neuropsychological testing, imaging, vestibular/vision/audiology/headache/seizure/mental health records as applicable.
Evaluate DC 8045 facets and any separately ratable residuals.
Compare current findings to prior baseline.
frequency_duration_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and functional loss of residual episodes since last evaluation.
Daily living limits: reminders, finances, medications, appointments, driving/navigation, household tasks, childcare, social relationships.
Impact of headache/dizziness/fatigue episodes on work and ADLs.
severity_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and functional loss of residual episodes since last evaluation.
Daily living limits: reminders, finances, medications, appointments, driving/navigation, household tasks, childcare, social relationships.
flare_up_prompts: Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and functional loss of residual episodes since last evaluation.
Impact of headache/dizziness/fatigue episodes on work and ADLs.
functional_impact_prompts: Worsened work reliability, errors, absences, safety concerns, need for accommodations/supervision, reduced hours, or inability to perform prior tasks.
Daily living limits: reminders, finances, medications, appointments, driving/navigation, household tasks, childcare, social relationships.
Impact of headache/dizziness/fatigue episodes on work and ADLs.
Relationship and behavioral consequences such as conflict, isolation, anger, or impaired judgment.

sections.title: SECTION 1 - SOURCE AND AVAILABILITY
items: VA lists this DBQ as not available for public use.
Use this JSON as a preparation aid, not as a completed DBQ.

sections.title: SECTION 2 - DIAGNOSIS / CURRENT CONDITION
items: Identify already service-connected TBI and all current residuals since the last exam/rating.
Describe worsening, improvement, new residuals, treatment changes, medications, rehab, hospitalizations, and new testing.
Differentiate TBI residuals from unrelated conditions when possible, while noting overlap that requires examiner judgment.

sections.title: SECTION 3 - MEDICAL HISTORY AND NEXUS FACTS
items: Timeline since last VA exam/rating: symptoms, treatment, testing, work changes, accidents, hospitalizations, and new diagnoses.
For increases/reviews, focus on current severity and progression; nexus may already be established unless a new residual or secondary issue is claimed.

sections.title: SECTION 4 - SYMPTOMS, SIGNS, AND FINDINGS
items: Change in memory, concentration, executive function, judgment, orientation, social interaction, visual-spatial skills, communication, or consciousness.
Headaches, dizziness, vertigo, balance problems, sleep disturbance, fatigue, tinnitus, light/noise sensitivity, vision symptoms.
Irritability, impulsivity, mood swings, anxiety/depression, aggression, apathy, social withdrawal, or inappropriate behavior.
Seizures, motor/sensory deficits, gait/coordination issues, speech/swallowing problems, autonomic/endocrine residuals.
Frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and functional loss of residual episodes since last evaluation.

sections.title: SECTION 5 - TESTING / OBJECTIVE FINDINGS
items: Review prior TBI exam/rating, current treatment records, neuropsychological testing, imaging, vestibular/vision/audiology/headache/seizure/mental health records as applicable.
Evaluate DC 8045 facets and any separately ratable residuals.
Compare current findings to prior baseline.

sections.title: SECTION 6 - FUNCTIONAL IMPACT
items: Worsened work reliability, errors, absences, safety concerns, need for accommodations/supervision, reduced hours, or inability to perform prior tasks.
Daily living limits: reminders, finances, medications, appointments, driving/navigation, household tasks, childcare, social relationships.
Impact of headache/dizziness/fatigue episodes on work and ADLs.
Relationship and behavioral consequences such as conflict, isolation, anger, or impaired judgment.

sections.title: SECTION 7 - SPECIAL NOTES
items: Avoid attributing all symptoms to TBI without medical support; note uncertainty for examiner review.

recommended_user_inputs: Current diagnosis or claimed symptom/residual pattern
Exact onset date/timeframe and in-service event/exposure/injury/captivity/deployment/acoustic/blast facts
Treatment history, medications, devices, therapy, surgeries, hospitalizations, specialist care, tests, and records
Baseline symptoms with frequency, duration, severity, and daily functional impact
Flare-ups/episodes/attacks with triggers, duration, severity, and what activities become impossible or limited
Work impact, accommodations, missed work, safety issues, communication limits, memory/concentration issues, ADL limits, and relationship effects
cheat_sheet_use.truthfulness_guardrail: Use only accurate personal facts. Do not invent symptoms, diagnoses, stressors, captivity details, exposures, test results, or medical nexus opinions.

script_templates.truthfulness_guardrail: Fill in only what is true. Skip wording that does not apply. Do not exaggerate, invent symptoms, stressors, exposures, test results, or medical nexus opinions.

default_options.duration: hours at a time, with bad episodes affecting the rest of the day
default_options.triggers: screens, reading, bright light, noise, crowds, stress, poor sleep, driving, multitasking, or physical exertion
default_options.limitation: memory, concentration, task completion, driving, reading/screens, balance, headaches, communication, mood, and work reliability
default_options.treatment: TBI/neurology care, primary care, headache medication if prescribed, therapy/rehab, memory aids, sleep treatment, vestibular/vision therapy if prescribed, and pacing
default_options.flareCare: stop the task, reduce light/noise, rest in a quiet place, use medication if prescribed, use notes/reminders, avoid driving, and ask for help if needed
default_options.limitBeforeStopping: 20–30 minutes of screens, reading, multitasking, or busy/noisy settings
default_options.serviceEvent: [previously documented TBI/head injury and any worsening, new residuals, or continued symptoms since the last exam]
default_options.workImpact: Since the last review, I still need written instructions, repeat reminders, reduced distractions, extra time for multi-step tasks, and breaks from screens/noise; interruptions can cause mistakes or missed steps.
default_options.dailyImpact: Since the last review, I still rely on phone alarms, notes, GPS, calendars, and routines; I may forget appointments, misplace items, lose my place in conversations, avoid night/busy driving, and need quiet rest after headaches or dizziness.
default_options.mainSymptoms: current memory lapses, poor concentration, slowed thinking, headaches, dizziness, light/noise sensitivity, irritability, sleep disruption, balance problems, and fatigue if true
default_options.evidence: service records, treatment records, VA/private records, medications, imaging/labs/testing, profiles, buddy statements, or lay statements if true
default_options.aliases: TBI traumatic brain injury concussion head injury blast injury review evaluation residuals DC 8045 cognitive impairment worsening

sections.title: Medical History / Onset & Nexus
sections.intent: Answer when/where symptoms started, what service event/exposure/aggravation may connect them, and what treatment has been tried.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: current diagnosis or claimed condition, onset, course, treatment history, medications, testing, and current symptoms; in-service event, injury, illness, hazardous exposure, aggravation, or secondary service connection timeline; treatment records, medications, tests, imaging/labs, specialist care, therapy, devices, and supporting evidence
sections.what_to_say_template: My claimed condition is {{currentDiagnosis}}. It first started or became noticeable {{onset}} after {{serviceEvent}}. Since then, the condition has continued with {{mainSymptoms}}. I have tried {{treatment}}, but symptoms still happen {{frequency}}. Evidence that may support the timeline includes {{evidence}}.

bullet_templates: TBI event: blast, fall, head strike, vehicle accident, concussion, loss/altered consciousness, or memory gap
Immediate symptoms: confusion, dazed feeling, headache, dizziness, nausea, vomiting, ringing ears, vision changes, or amnesia
Testing/treatment: CT/MRI, neuropsych testing, neurology/TBI clinic, vestibular/vision therapy, headache treatment
Timeline: what changed since the injury or since the last TBI review exam

sections.title: Symptom Evaluation
sections.intent: Answer DBQ-style symptom questions with exact frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and condition-specific symptoms.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: frequency, duration, severity, flare-ups, treatment, work impact, and daily activity limits
sections.what_to_say_template: My symptoms are {{mainSymptoms}}. They happen {{frequency}}, usually last {{duration}}, and are triggered or worsened by {{triggers}}. On better days they are about {{bestPain}}; on worst days they reach {{worstPain}}. For example, memory, focus, headache, dizziness, or brain-fog symptoms happen {{frequency}}, worsen with {{triggers}}, and can last {{duration}}.

subsections.title: Frequency & Duration
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Document frequency, duration, pattern, triggers, and whether symptoms are constant, intermittent, recurrent, or episodic.
subsections.what_to_say_template: My {{currentDiagnosis}} symptoms are {{frequency}}. They usually last {{duration}}. The symptoms I need documented are {{mainSymptoms}}. They get worse with {{triggers}}.
subsections.example_template: For example, memory, focus, headache, dizziness, or brain-fog symptoms happen {{frequency}}, worsen with {{triggers}}, and can last {{duration}}.

subsections.title: Severity Scale
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe severity using measurable words, worst-day impairment, and any DBQ-specific severe-episode language that truthfully applies.
subsections.what_to_say_template: On better days, severity is about {{bestPain}}. On worst days, it reaches {{worstPain}}. Overall, I would describe it as {{severity}} because it interferes with {{limitation}}.
subsections.example_template: On a bad day, symptoms reach {{worstPain}} and I have to stop or reduce {{limitation}} instead of pushing through.

bullet_templates: Cognitive facets: memory, attention, concentration, executive function, judgment, orientation
Daily examples: forget appointments, repeat questions, lose items, miss steps, rely on notes/alarms/GPS
Subjective residuals: headaches, dizziness/vertigo, tinnitus, light/noise sensitivity, sleep disruption, fatigue
Neurobehavioral/communication: irritability, impulsivity, social withdrawal, word-finding, trouble following conversations

sections.title: Functional Impact / Occupational Assessment
sections.intent: Answer how the disability limits work, daily activities, relationships/social function, reliability, and worst-day functioning.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: frequency, duration, severity, flare-ups, treatment, work impact, and daily activity limits

subsections.title: Daily Activities
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Explain work limitations, daily activity limits, missed time, accommodations, safety concerns, and reduced reliability/productivity.
subsections.what_to_say_template: This affects work because {{workImpact}} It affects daily life because {{dailyImpact}} I can usually continue for about {{limitBeforeStopping}} before I need to stop, rest, modify the task, or get help.
subsections.example_template: A real-life example is: I need written instructions, reminders, fewer distractions, extra time, and breaks from screens/noise because interruptions or multitasking can cause missed steps and mistakes.

subsections.title: Flare-Ups / Worst Days
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe flare-up frequency, triggers, duration, worst-day functional loss, and what helps relieve symptoms.
subsections.what_to_say_template: Flare-ups or worst episodes happen {{flareUps}}. They are triggered by {{triggers}} and can last {{duration}}. During them, I cannot keep up with {{limitation}}, and I use {{flareCare}}.
subsections.example_template: During a flare-up, headache, dizziness, light/noise sensitivity, or brain fog worsens to {{worstPain}}, I stop screens/driving, rest in a quiet place, use notes/reminders, and may need help with tasks or transportation.

bullet_templates: Work: written instructions, repeated reminders, extra time, reduced distractions, screen/noise breaks, missed steps/mistakes
Driving/safety: dizziness, getting lost, slow reaction, avoiding night/busy traffic, needing GPS even on familiar routes
Home/IADLs: bills, meds, appointments, cooking steps, chores, conversations, childcare, errands
Worst days: headache/brain fog/dizziness forces dark quiet room, no driving, no screens, help from family/coworker
